fills: Tabel 4.7 / T8e + Gambar 4.8a-c — deployed read-out: per dataset x window HCIE(kalman) vs BKT/DKT/SAKT/IRT, + BKT-floor Simpson verdict
predictor_definition: HCIE-deployed: lagged 2-learner Kalman+Bayesian mean; cold-start = leak-free per-concept population prior (md5(user)%2 split). NOTE: this is the deployed lagged read-out per the artifact, evaluated cross-dataset; canonical of-record seal is single-Kalman m_K.
auc_method: dependency-free rank-AUC, averaged-rank ties, matched-intersection events
framing: AUC COMPETITIVE not superior. HCIE leads on the CS / programming domain (CSEDM) and on EdNet (vs BKT), is COMPETITIVE-band vs IRT, and reads BELOW the deep models on Junyi/ASSISTments-2015 at the pooled level. The BKT-floor / Simpson signal is concrete: across every dataset the cold-start sub-population is where HCIE's lift sits (hcie_cold > hcie_warm in most cells) while pooled deep models can lead — pooled<deep but sub-pops win is the documented Simpson story. Per-dataset governance/JT not involved here (pure KT read-out).

csedm_f19.label: CSEDM F19 (programming logs)
csedm_f19.bkt_floor_note: BKT cold AUC = 0.5 (floor: BKT has no signal on cold-start users); HCIE cold = 0.697 — HCIE's edge is concentrated in the cold sub-population.

junyi_2015.label: Junyi 2015 (Phase 1, no graph)
junyi_2015.bkt_floor_note: Pooled HCIE below deep models, BUT sub-pop decomp: hcie_warm 0.754 > bkt_warm 0.721, and hcie_cold 0.763 > bkt_cold 0.5 (floor). Classic Simpson: HCIE leads BKT in BOTH sub-pops yet pooled below the high-N deep models.

ednet_kt1.label: EdNet KT1
ednet_kt1.bkt_floor_note: HCIE beats BKT overall (+0.0287); BKT cold = 0.5 floor, HCIE cold = 0.673. Deep models (DKT/SAKT) lead on this dense dataset.

assistments_2015.label: ASSISTments 2015
assistments_2015.bkt_floor_note: Pooled HCIE below BKT/DKT/SAKT, BEATS IRT. Decomp: hcie_warm 0.643 > bkt_warm 0.635 and hcie_cold 0.618 > bkt_cold 0.5 (floor) — HCIE leads BKT in both sub-pops, pooled HCIE below BKT (Simpson again, driven by warm/cold mix differences).

bkt_floor_simpson_verdict: BKT cold-start AUC is the 0.5 FLOOR on every dataset (CSEDM/Junyi/EdNet/ASSIST-2015) — vanilla BKT has no signal on never-seen learners. HCIE's cold-start sub-population AUC is well above floor everywhere (0.62-0.76). On CSEDM and EdNet HCIE BEATS BKT pooled; on Junyi and ASSIST-2015 HCIE BEATS BKT in BOTH warm and cold sub-populations yet reads below BKT pooled (textbook Simpson's paradox from sub-population mixing). Headline: pooled deep-model lead on the dense datasets is a Simpson artifact, not a per-learner loss — HCIE's structural edge is the cold-start sub-population where BKT floors out.

auxiliary_small_sample_runs.note: Two tiny GKT-only matched-eval runs are in the same artifact but FLAGGED, never headlined (<=5 users): junyi_gkt_paired (run-4f72c294, n=69) and junyi_gkt_only (run-0307bda5, n=13). Excluded from the Tabel 4.7 cells per their small_window_caveat.
